criteria.requirement: Every research area that qualifies for this project — platform feasibility, licensing, external APIs, migrations, integrations, and load-bearing performance or security — is addressed; a qualifying area is never silently dropped.
criteria.pass: Each of the six canonical areas is either investigated or explicitly marked not-applicable, so a reader can confirm nothing was skipped by accident.
criteria.fail: A qualifying area the build depends on (for example an external API in the deliverable) is absent with no note explaining why it went uninvestigated.

criteria.requirement: Each addressed area names the concrete assumption the build depends on, stated specifically enough to be right or wrong.
criteria.pass: Every area carries a falsifiable assumption a reader could confirm or refute ('the target platform ships Node 20', not 'the platform is fine').
criteria.fail: An area gestures at a topic without stating what is actually assumed, so the assumption cannot be checked.

criteria.requirement: Each assumption is backed by cited evidence, or — when evidence could not be obtained — the uncertainty is recorded explicitly rather than papered over.
criteria.pass: Every assumption carries either a concrete source a reader could follow or a plainly labelled unknown; a failed investigation is recorded as an honest gap.
criteria.fail: An assumption is presented as settled with neither a cited source nor an acknowledgement that it stays unverified.

criteria.requirement: Each area states its reversibility cost: what undoing the choice would take if the assumption proves wrong.
criteria.pass: Every area names the cost of reversing course (a config swap, a schema migration, vendor lock-in), so a decision-maker can weigh the downside.
criteria.fail: An area commits to a direction without saying how hard it would be to reverse should the assumption fail.

criteria.requirement: No claim is asserted with more certainty than its evidence or recorded uncertainty earns.
criteria.pass: Each claim's confidence matches its backing — verified facts read as facts, open questions read as open.
criteria.fail: A claim carries a certainty its evidence does not support, or an open question is dressed up as resolved.
